pilot_evaluation.reason: Uncertain topology or borderline metrics - Manual calibration needed.

model_policy_reason: Production Policy v1: Haiku-only due to graph density ceiling

questions.question: What is the purpose of USB device classes?
questions.justification: The synthesis text states that USB device classes are a mechanism to classify USB devices with similar functionality into the same class, which can simplify host-side management and support.

questions.question: What are some common USB device classes?
questions.justification: The synthesis text lists several common USB device classes, including Human Interface Devices (HID), Physical Interface Devices (PID), Monitor Control, Power Devices, and Point of Sale (POS) devices.

questions.question: How do HID devices communicate with the host?
questions.justification: The synthesis text explains that HID devices typically use two communication methods: control transfers for feature reports, and interrupt transfers for input and output reports.
